implementation.canonical_complete_cell_path: Before the generic tag parser, the bounded reader recognizes exact writer-compatible complete value, formula, cached-formula and simple inline-string cells and emits one callback-lifetime complete-cell event over the exact source span.
implementation.safe_fallback: Attribute variants, rich or unsupported metadata, malformed markup and reader-window splits continue through the prior structural parser and retain its diagnostics.
implementation.audit_contract: The direct event retains coordinate, formula, shared-string, style and inline-string audit metadata. The transformer still owns replacement lookup, dimension extrema, relationship scanning and pass-through batching.
implementation.lifetime_and_memory: The path returns string_view data only for the current bounded callback, copies no cell payload and retains no view after window consumption.
implementation.telemetry: Schema v13 adds canonical complete-cell count, bytes, formula count and inline-string count. Matrix v7 validates positive canonical traffic and profile-specific type counters while preserving every schema-v12/v6 field.

compatibility_and_memory.representative_openpyxl: opened with openpyxl 3.1.2 for all six optimized matrices

interpretation.supported_claim: Across both execution orders and byte-identical one-million-cell sources, schema v13 reduced the measured transform and source scan/action medians for numeric, mixed-inline and formula-plus-hyperlink workloads. The deterministic parser-event reduction was 73.53% to 76.92%, without increasing source callback or transform action traffic.
interpretation.total_editor_policy: No generic total-editor reduction is claimed. Package-writer and temporary-file timing varied with execution order; numeric total improved in the optimized-first round but regressed 5.51% in the baseline-first round even though its transform and source scan/action stages improved in both rounds.
interpretation.memory: Optimized representative peak working set remained between 8.58203 MB and 9.58594 MB and did not exceed its paired baseline median in either execution order.
interpretation.compatibility: Representative outputs opened with openpyxl 3.1.2, and both optimized formula matrices preserved the external hyperlink target.
interpretation.next_hotspot: After reducing generic cell parsing, the recorded single-pass source scan remains material while package target-entry and temporary-file timing can dominate total elapsed. The next pass should profile their handoff and IO behavior under an isolated host before changing compression or transaction semantics.

scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, deterministic one-million-cell 10-column sources, 1000 targeted upserts, source DEFLATE level 6 and output DEFLATE level 1. Claims are limited to the recorded stage medians and traffic counters and do not generalize to arbitrary workbooks, Office, cross-machine performance, full-feature superiority or OpenXLSX superiority.
